challenges.title: Typed greeting
challenges.description: Write a function `greet(name: string): string` that returns the string 'Hello, <name>!'.

steps.title: Understand the signature
steps.explanation: The function takes a string parameter and must return a string. TypeScript enforces these types at compile time.

steps.title: Use a template literal
steps.explanation: Template literals (backtick strings) allow embedding expressions with ${...}.

challenges.title: Generic identity function
challenges.description: Write a generic function `identity<T>(value: T): T` that simply returns its argument unchanged.
challenges.hint: Generic functions use angle-bracket syntax <T>. The type parameter T is inferred from the argument.

steps.title: Understand generics
steps.explanation: A generic parameter <T> lets the function work with any type while still preserving type information.

steps.title: Return the value
steps.explanation: The function just returns its argument. TypeScript infers T from the call site.

steps.title: Verify
steps.explanation: identity(42) returns 42 (number). identity('hi') returns 'hi' (string). The return type matches the input type.

challenges.title: Readonly deep freeze
challenges.description: Write a type utility `DeepReadonly<T>` that recursively makes all properties of an object type readonly.
challenges.hint: Use a mapped type with readonly and a conditional type to recurse into object properties.

steps.title: Mapped type
steps.explanation: Use [K in keyof T] to iterate over every property key of T.

steps.title: Add readonly
steps.explanation: Prefix with readonly to make each property immutable.

steps.title: Recurse into objects
steps.explanation: Use a conditional type to apply DeepReadonly recursively when the property value is itself an object.

challenges.title: Type-safe event emitter
challenges.description: Implement a type-safe `EventEmitter<Events>` class where `Events` is a record mapping event names to their payload types. The class must have `on(event, listener)` and `emit(event, payload)` methods with full type inference.
challenges.hint: Use a Map keyed by event name. Make `on` and `emit` generic over the key K extends keyof Events.

steps.title: Store listeners in a Map
steps.explanation: A Map<keyof Events, listener[]> lets us look up handlers for each event name in O(1).

steps.title: Type-safe on()
steps.explanation: Make K a type parameter constrained to keyof Events. The listener receives Events[K], so TypeScript enforces the correct payload type at every call site.

steps.title: Type-safe emit()
steps.explanation: emit() also constrains payload to Events[K], ensuring you can't emit the wrong type for a given event.
